nameMeanings.name: Stedman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Steadman
meanings: occupational or topographic name from Middle English stede ‘place, farm, estate’ (Old English stede) + -man, used for someone who lived or worked on a farm, or who lived at a place so named. Compare Stead .
occupational name for someone who worked with horses, from Middle English sted(e) ‘horse, stallion’ (Old English steda) + -man. Compare Steed .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Stedman was born in 1532, in Kinross-shire, Scotland, his father, Alexander Barton Steedman, was 22 and his mother, Lady Christian Stuart, was 22. He married Countesses Margaret Anderson of Angus in 1554, in Kinross-shire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 22 August 1606, in his hometown, at the age of 74.

Il a épousé Countesses Margaret Anderson of Angus en 1554, à Kinross-shire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 22 août 1606, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 74 ans.
